history.content:
<thought>
I see that the goal G is two cells ahead and two cells to the right in the local map. The forward cell in front of me is a floor cell, so I can move forward safely. Moving forward will bring me closer to the goal while avoiding lava. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and one cell to the right of my current position. However, the two cells right in front of me are lava cells, which are deadly. I cannot move forward safely, so I should turn right to try to find an alternative path around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward with a wall directly in front of me, so I cannot move forward. The goal cell G is visible to my left and slightly forward, adjacent to a lava cell L. To reach the goal safely, I should turn left to face that direction and then plan to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and one cell to the right of my current position. However, the cell right in front of me is lava, which is deadly. Moving forward is not safe. I will turn right to avoid the lava and try to take another path towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible slightly to my left and ahead. There is a lava cell L directly to the left of the goal. The cell immediately in front of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. To reach the goal safely, I need to turn left first to face toward the goal and then move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal (G) is visible two cells diagonally forward and to the right. However, the cells directly in front and to the right are lava (L), which are deadly. The cell directly forward is also lava, so moving forward is unsafe. Turning right leads to lava as well, so I should try turning left to explore another direction and avoid lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward with a wall directly ahead. Moving forward is not possible. I should turn right or left to find a free path. Since both sides seem to have walls extending, I will arbitrarily turn right to explore.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see that there is goal G two cells diagonally to the right and ahead. Directly in front of me are lava cells, which I cannot move into. To reach the goal, I need to change my facing direction first. Turning right will face me towards the free floor cells, allowing me to navigate safely around the lava towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
My current position is at the bottom-center of the view, facing upwards. Directly ahead is a wall cell '#', so I cannot move forward. To the left, there is a goal cell 'G' adjacent to lava 'L', but there is a path to the left of my position that might be safe to navigate around the lava. I will turn left to explore the open cell to the left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Ahead of me are two lava cells blocking forward movement. The goal 'G' is visible two cells ahead and two cells to the right, but the path directly ahead is blocked by lava. The left and right parts of the view are also blocked by walls ('#'). To find a safe path, I will turn right to explore an alternative route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal cell G two cells ahead and one cell to the left. However, there is a wall (#) directly in front of me. I need to turn to find a path towards the goal. Turning left will allow me to face the open floor cells that lead to the goal without running into walls or lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and two cells to the right. However, the two cells directly in front of me contain lava (L), which are deadly and must be avoided. I see walls to the left side, so turning left might give an open path. I will turn left to explore the safer direction and try to find a path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see a wall directly in front of me ('#'). I cannot move forward, so I need to turn to find a free path. I will turn left to check if there is a way to move there safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so moving forward is not possible. I will need to turn to find a navigable path. Turning right might give a path around the wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward at an empty cell ('.') right in front of me. There are walls '#' to my immediate left and right, so moving forward is safe and the only option. Since the goal or dangerous cells are not visible, moving forward to explore seems best.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
